question: What is the definition of the abbreviation RPV?

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. It employs passive safety systems—including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems—that do not rely on electrical power, with the reactor and containment partially immersed in water to provide an effective passive heat sink. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron, and its modular construction enables in-shop fabrication of major components.

nuscale_ch01_0018.title: Principal Design Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0018.summary: The section describes the principal design criteria for the NuScale plant, focusing on the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) and the overall facility process. It outlines the reactor core’s heat transfer process, natural coolant circulation, steam generation, and the flow of superheated steam to the turbine and condenser, emphasizing the system’s role in power generation and heat removal.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis report diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system status, control, and maintenance. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale's Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices. It includes symbols for sample probes, pressure and temperature measurement devices, flow meters, level indicators, and analysis accessories, ensuring standardized interpretation of the diagrams throughout the safety analysis report.

nuscale_ch01_0005.title: Reactor Type
nuscale_ch01_0005.summary: The NuScale reactor is a small modular pressurized water reactor (PWR) featuring a cylindrical steel containment vessel designed to ASME standards, with internal pressure maintained at a vacuum during normal operation. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, uses natural circulation for cooling (eliminating the need for pumps), and is rated at 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), with up to 12 modules per plant for a total net output of approximately 570 MWe.

nuscale_ch01_0007.title: Schedule
nuscale_ch01_0007.summary: The NuScale containment vessel is a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and operates under vacuum during normal conditions. The reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor with natural circulation, eliminating the need for coolant pumps and external piping. Each module produces about 50 MWe, with a 12-module plant yielding a net output of approximately 570 MWe, and applicants referencing this design must provide their own construction and operation schedules.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in compliance with regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section B defines RPV as the lower assembly housing the reactor core, while Section A references RPV in the context of penetrations, thus Section B provides the specific meaning of RPV used in Section A.
Section A and Section B both provide legend information for interpreting symbols and abbreviations in NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report figures, but neither defines the abbreviation RPV.
Section B defines RPV as the lower reactor pressure vessel assembly, while Section A does not mention or define RPV, so Section B provides the relevant definition.

Section A provides general information about the structure and referencing of tables and figures in the FSAR, while Section B is an example of a specific figure (Figure 1.7-2) that follows this referencing system.
